arts.ttl: Elanga scores again as Forest beat Spurs to go THIRD
arts.sum: Anthony Elanga scored for the third Premier League match in a row to move Nottingham Forest up to third as they beat Tottenham Hotspur 1-0 at the City Ground. After a slow start, which saw Ryan Yates booked for a foul on Son, meaning he will miss Forest’s trip to Goodison Park to face Everton on Sunday, they eventually found their groove when Elanga tucked home his third Premier League goal in as many matches in the competition.

arts.ttl: Spurs players ‘giving everything’ as Forest deliver latest blow
arts.sum: Spurs boss Ange Postecoglou says his players are “giving everything” on the pitch after falling to defeat at Nottingham Forest. Tottenham enjoyed the majority of possession and took more shots than their opponents, but Anthony Elanga’s first-half goal was the difference between the two sides at the City Ground.
